artifacts.summary: Research report clarifying domain semantics: CanonicalMCS as world states vs Int as time indices, identifying conceptual confusions in codebase

next_steps: Run /plan 15 to create revised implementation plan addressing domain separation

key_findings: CanonicalMCS is world state space (MCS), NOT time index domain
Int/TimelineQuot is correct time index domain with AddCommGroup
BFMCS encodes space of world histories, parametric in D (time domain)
FMCS CanonicalMCS is proof technique, not semantic model
W=D conflation marked deprecated in DurationTransfer.lean
SuccChainBFMCS.lean should be boneyarded (singleton modal_backward impossible)
Phase 4 gap: wiring MCS-level saturation to BFMCS Int structure
comments_needing_fixes: CanonicalDomain.lean: world states are also D comment
